answers: A credit score is a numerical rating (typically 300-850) that represents your creditworthiness based on your credit history, payment behavior, and debt levels.
Credit score holo tomar financial trustworthiness er number. 750+ ke excellent dhora hoy. Eta loan approval, interest rate, ar credit card pawar jonno important.
To improve your credit score, pay bills on time, keep credit utilization below 30%, don't close old accounts, avoid too many new credit applications, and check your report for errors.
Credit scores are calculated using payment history (35%), credit utilization (30%), length of credit history (15%), credit mix (10%), and new credit inquiries (10%).
